results.name: BHP Group Ltd
results.confidence_reason: BHP is a top-tier diversified miner with fortress-like coverage metrics, strong operating cash generation, and modest leverage even after recent debt increase.
results.primary_driver: No distress evident. This is a well-capitalized major with interest coverage above 28x and net debt at just 0.69x EBITDA. results.name: Bank of Queensland Ltd
results.z_score_zone: N/A — Z-score not applicable to financials
results.confidence_reason: This is a regulated bank; the distressed ratios reflect banking business model characteristics (low current ratio due to deposit funding, high leverage inherent to banking), not financial distress.
results.primary_driver: The distress signals are false positives. Banks operate with structural leverage and maturity transformation by design. The 0.05 current ratio reflects deposits (short-term liabilities) funding loans (long-term assets), which is normal. Interest coverage of 0.13x appears problematic only because net interest income accounting differs from industrial firms—banks report net interest margin, not gross interest expense ratios. The Altman Z-Score is explicitly invalid for financials.
results.trend: Core profitability has stabilized after a FY2025 H1 loss, with EBIT recovering to $210m in the latest period.
